gauge.name: Attoyac Bayou near Chireno

impacts.statement: Expect flooding along several thousands of acres of the entire length of Attoyac Bayou to continue for several days.
Expect moderate to severe lowland flooding of timber in and near the river floodplain. The bayou will continue to remain above flood stage for several days.
The bayou will continue to slowly fall during the next several days, and lowland flooding will gradually decrease.
Expect minor lowland flooding of boat ramps and pastures.  Move livestock and equipment to higher ground.
Expect flooded paths and secondary roadways.  Move livestock and equipment to higher ground.
Expect lowland flooding for the next several days of the heavily wooded floodplain. Ranchers that have cattle and equipment near the river should move them to higher ground.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
